localizations.title: Ticket to Ride
localizations.hiddenObject_2: Look at the dark part between cars 17 and 19.
localizations.hint_4: You have already seen these shoes.
localizations.hint_7: This overexcited young dog needs any pretext to sweep his master along.
localizations.hint_9: During this adventure, you will have to photograph several details on various cards. The reporter's eye will guide you!
localizations.hint_10: There is a hidden number on this matchbook.
localizations.hint_12: Take a picture of this foot before his owner flees.
localizations.hint_14: You must find a way to create a diversion to get to this briefcase!
localizations.hint_15: While admiring this majestic eagle, you tell yourself how ideal this wide-open ventilation duct could be for creating a diversion.
localizations.hint_16: Did you reveal cards 10 and 34? There are No hidden number(s) here.
localizations.hint_17: Did you join this card to card 19 to read the text? You must try to find ASAP what this strange noise between the cars is all about.
localizations.hint_18: Fix your gaze on the dark part that separates the two cars: do you see anything forming there?
localizations.hint_19: Is there anything else you could show to Grace and Walter? Also, you need to investigate this strange noise between the cars.
localizations.hint_20: Did you reveal cards R and K? On card R, the Salt Lake City-Las Vegas section is of a specific color. Did you see it elsewhere?
localizations.hint_21: Maybe one of the pictures you took will interest Grace?
localizations.hint_24: Who could be interested in the picture of the eagle?
localizations.hint_31: Only one of these topics will interest your editor-in-chief. The title of this adventure may put you on the right track.
localizations.hint_33: How can you gain access to this locomotive? You may want to go back to the front car to get closer to it.
localizations.hint_34: Did you notice the red lipstick smudge on the cup? But don't get fooled… the cookies are what's important here.
localizations.hint_36: Did you reveal cards 17 and 19? What will Ms. Smith do with this chloroform? No hidden number(s) here.
localizations.hint_39: Walter is fond of birds. You may want to immortalize one.
localizations.hint_40: Do you have any evidence you could use with the involved people?
localizations.hint_41: You are a reporter, aren't you? So what are you waiting for? Take a picture of the exchange between the man and the woman.
localizations.hint_42: Haven't you crossed paths with this red-hair woman?
localizations.hint_46: This is an old newspaper. It's only worth burning…
localizations.hint_47: Thick smoke rises from the newspaper. It could help create a diversion.
localizations.hint_49: Only one of the three topics of card 31 will interest your editor-in-chief. The title of this adventure may put you on the right track.
localizations.hint_51: Did you make sure to place card 7 to the right of this one? A scene in the background catches your attention. Take a picture.
localizations.hint_53: Interesting: according to this agreement, Roy's PINK lines make only one with Walter's yellow lines.
localizations.hint_62: You need to get more information from Walter to be able to open this briefcase.
localizations.hint_63: This story seems mundane, but for Walter, it is an unforgettable one. Some keywords could help you open his briefcase.
localizations.hint_64: This part of the map shows the first section of the route (Salt Lake City): place card 95 horizontally to its right. Then place card 78 horizontally below cards 64 and 95 to recreate the map.
localizations.hint_72: Did you reveal cards 14, 15, 16, and B? No hidden number(s) here.
localizations.hint_74: There are two potential paths from Saint Louis without taking the green path: count the number of rectangles for each route.
localizations.hint_78: This part of the map shows the middle section of the route: it is placed horizontally below the two others. Once the map is recreated, enter machine 20 (locomotive) to access the commands…
localizations.hint_80: Did you reveal cards 9, 86, and T? No hidden number(s) here.
localizations.hint_82: Take a picture of the bottle sticking out of the bag before the woman disappears…
localizations.hint_86: Some of your cards bear the first letters of the route.
localizations.hint_87: 1, 3, 6, 10, 15... How many bridges will form the next pyramid?
localizations.hint_95: This part of the map is the last section of the route (Salt Lake City-Las Vegas). Place it horizontally to the right of card 64.
localizations.hint_97: Did you reveal cards 31 and 49? No hidden number(s) here.
localizations.hint_98: You must find seat 57, which is probably in one of the next cars.
localizations.hint_111: You will have no time to read the document before Walter's henchmen return… So how can you capture it?
localizations.hint_222: The vowels are missing from this message.
localizations.answer_hint_1: Set the newspaper (46) on fire with this match (1) = 46+1=47.
localizations.answer_hint_7: To see the other end of the platform, toss the cookies (34) to the dog (7): 34+7=41
localizations.answer_hint_19: Show the picture (4) to Grace and Walter (19): 4+19=23.
localizations.answer_hint_20: Take the three cards with the backs depicting a blue wagon.
localizations.answer_hint_21: Show the picture of the agreement (53) to Grace (21) to provoke a reaction: 53+21=74.
localizations.answer_hint_31: Submit your article on Carrie Baker's demise (+31) to your editor-in-chief (49): 49+31=80.
localizations.answer_hint_33: 14, 15, 16, 17, 18, 19, the latter being the front car.
localizations.answer_hint_39: Take a picture of the eagle from cabin 15 (card 24) and show it to Walter: 24+39=63.
localizations.answer_hint_42: Show the picture of the exchange (40) to Ms. Smith (42): 40+42=82.
localizations.answer_hint_47: Place the newspaper (47) next to the ventilation duct of cabin 15 to fill the next-door cabin with smoke: 47+15= 62.
localizations.answer_hint_49: Submit your article on Carrie Baker's demise (+31) to your editor-in-chief (49): 49+31=80.
localizations.answer_hint_53: The yellow and pink lines on card M form a 51.
localizations.answer_hint_62: The story on card 63 provides the keywords: HUNTERS, DEER, LEAF, then PARTRIDGE. Enter these symbols into the app.
localizations.answer_hint_74: The shortest path is Saint Louis / Omaha / Kansas City / Oklahoma City / Dallas / Santa, totaling 12 rectangles.
localizations.answer_hint_86: The first letters of the route are NY-LA.
localizations.answer_hint_87: We add a base of 6 bridges to the preceding pyramid: 15+6=21.
localizations.answer_hint_98: Seats 41 to 50 are in car 71, and seats 51 to 60 are in car 72.
localizations.answer_hint_222: "Unlock speed: all buttons except white." This instruction will be useful once you gain access to the locomotive commands.
localizations.machine_20:
You manage to stop the train at the very last moment possible before Las Vegas. It never crossed your mind that one day you would be a hero.
But you can't rest on your laurels: you still need to write your article. The boss does not wait!
localizations.machine_62: You hear an inviting click, and the suitcase opens.
localizations.machine_9_event1:
All on board.
Departure for Santa Fe.
localizations.machine_9_event2:
All on board.
Departure for Los Angeles.
localizations.machine_20_wheel: The notch changer controlling the speed is unblocked.
localizations.end_answer_26: Did you take into account the duration of the stop offs?
localizations.end_error: No, this is not the correct answer.
localizations.machine_9_on_80:
Don't you have better things to do than taking pictures of me?
:Go right now to Grand Central.
localizations.machine_20_wheel_hint: The wheel is stuck. If only the engine driver could help you…
localizations.end_question_1: Tell me again how it went. To start with, how long was your trip from New York to Las Vegas?
localizations.end_question_2: Which tycoon initially held the majority?
localizations.end_question_4: What pen name do you wish to use for signing your article?
localizations.end_newspaper:
This commemorative railroad trip will be remembered in more ways than one, first, for having set a new record for crossing the continent, more specifically on the Salt Lake City - Las Vegas section, where the limits have been brilliantly surpassed.

But it is probably for the consequences on the railroad industry that we will remember this trip. With the majority at the start of the trip, Grace Baker, the company owner, almost had her monopoly stolen from her when her brother and uncle made a deal.

The uncle, Walter Baker, succeeded nevertheless in finding common ground with his niece. More specifically, after our team of reporters managed to uncover and thwart sabotage, orchestrated by no other than the brother, Roy Baker, and a mercenary going by the name of Ms. Smith.

Roy Baker’s intentions remain to this day unclear: riddled with debts, he engaged in double-dealing with his family, trying to take advantage of the situation, and would have probably inherited a substantial empire had his plan succeeded. He was seen for the last time fleeing Santa Fe, heading to El Paso.

What will this family saga bring us next?
localizations.end_signature:
For the New York Times,
The Railraod Adventurers:
